highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 advances the engine to Skia m151 and delivers broad managed-code performance improvements.
highlights_body: This release updates the bundled Skia engine to milestone m151 (with upstream bug-fix syncs), moves several hot-path type conversions and parsers into allocation-free managed C# code, adds .NET 11 WASM support via Emscripten 5.0.6, and fixes a native resource leak in SKImage.FromEncodedData.

bullets.lead: Skia engine updated to milestone m151
bullets.detail: The bundled Skia engine has been advanced to Chrome milestone m151, incorporating upstream rendering improvements.
Multiple rounds of cherry-picked upstream bug fixes from the m151 branch have been merged, improving rendering correctness and stability.

bullets.lead: SKColor hex parsing improved with ReadOnlySpan overloads
bullets.detail: SKColor.Parse and TryParse now accept ReadOnlySpan<char>, and the hex-parsing implementation has been optimised to avoid allocations.

bullets.lead: Native resource leak in SKImage.FromEncodedData fixed
bullets.detail: SKImage.FromEncodedData(SKData, SKRectI) was leaking a native handle when a crop region was specified; the handle is now correctly released.

bullets.lead: Unnecessary unsafe modifier removed from GRContextOptions
bullets.detail: The unsafe modifier on GRContextOptions was redundant and has been removed, improving code clarity without any behaviour change.

bullets.lead: SKColor/SKColorF conversion ported to managed code
bullets.detail: The hot-path conversion between SKColor and SKColorF no longer crosses the P/Invoke boundary — it is now computed entirely in managed C#, reducing overhead on colour-heavy workloads.

bullets.lead: SKPMColor premultiply/unpremultiply ported to managed integer math
bullets.detail: Single-value SKPMColor premultiply and unpremultiply operations now run in managed integer arithmetic instead of calling native code, eliminating interop overhead on the critical path.
SKFourByteTag.Parse no longer allocates a temporary char[4] on each call; the implementation now uses stack-allocated storage.

bullets.lead: SKShaper glyph results read via zero-copy spans
bullets.detail: Reading glyph output from SKShaper now uses direct Span<T> access into the native buffer instead of copying, cutting allocation and copy overhead for text-shaping workloads.

bullets.lead: Emscripten 5.0.6 static libraries added for .NET 11 WASM
bullets.detail: Pre-built Emscripten 5.0.6 static libraries are now included, enabling SkiaSharp to target the .NET 11 WASM runtime.

bullets.lead: Skia submodule WASM preprocessor fix applied
bullets.detail: A redefinition of SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM in the Skia submodule has been dropped, resolving a build warning that could cause subtle WASM compilation issues.
A dedicated preview-.NET WASM test job now runs on each CI build to catch exception-handling regressions before they reach consumers.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Added Emscripten 5.0.6 WASM libraries for .NET 11 support, contributed the HarfBuzzSharp allocation-free UTF-8 encoding, and fixed the WASM SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM redefinition in the Skia submodule

preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.2.1: Preview 2 delivered managed-code performance wins across SKColor hex parsing, SKFourByteTag.Parse, SKShaper zero-copy glyph reads, and HarfBuzzSharp allocation-free encoding; also applied upstream m151 bug-fix syncs and removed the redundant unsafe modifier from GRContextOptions.
preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.1.1: Preview 1 opened the 4.151.0 line by updating the Skia engine to milestone m151 and applying the WASM SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM submodule fix.

harfbuzz_summary: HarfBuzzSharp 14.2.1 ships with two allocation-free performance improvements: Buffer.AddUtf8(string) now uses a pooled UTF-8 encoder instead of allocating per call, and Tag.Parse eliminates the per-call char[4] temporary.
